================================================================================
PROJECT SUMMARY: NEXUS AI - SMART CAMPUS ASSISTANT
================================================================================
Last Updated: June 10, 2026
Institution: Sir Syed University of Engineering and Technology, Karachi

================================================================================
1. PROJECT OVERVIEW
================================================================================

Nexus AI is a futuristic, intelligent digital campus ecosystem built for Sir 
Syed University. It combines modern web technologies with AI capabilities to 
provide students with an integrated platform for academic and social life.

Design Philosophy: Quantum/Cyberpunk aesthetic with glassmorphism UI, neon glow 
effects, and radial gradients.

================================================================================
2. CORE FEATURES & PAGES
================================================================================

2.1 LANDING PAGE (Landing.tsx)
- Public welcome page
- First touchpoint for users
- Route: /

2.2 AUTHENTICATION SYSTEM
- Login Page (Login.tsx) - Route: /login
- Signup Page (Signup.tsx) - Route: /signup
- Features: JWT-based authentication, Bcrypt password hashing
- Context: AuthContext.tsx manages user state globally
- Session storage: localStorage for tokens and user data
- Roles: student, faculty, admin

2.3 DASHBOARD (Dashboard.tsx) - Route: /dashboard [PROTECTED]
- Central hub displaying user statistics
- Stats tracked: Attendance (85%), Active Courses (6), Next Class time, Assignments (2 Due)
- Views: Daily, Courses, Assignments
- Displays course list with attendance tracking
- Shows upcoming assignments with due dates
- DB health status checker (/api/health endpoint)



2.5 STUDY HELPER (StudyHelper.tsx) - Route: /study-helper [PROTECTED]
- AI-powered study companion
- Features: Note summarization, study schedules, complex topic breakdown
- Component: Neural Study Helper

2.6 CAMPUS MAP (CampusMap.tsx) - Route: /map [PROTECTED]
- Interactive campus navigation system
- Features: Department locations, lab locations, facility finder
- Component: Campus Navigator

2.7 ANNOUNCEMENTS (Announcements.tsx) - Route: /announcements [PROTECTED]
- Real-time notification feed
- Features: University events, academic notices
- Component: Smart Announcements system

2.8 FACULTY DIRECTORY (Faculty.tsx) - Route: /faculty [PROTECTED]
- Professor and staff search/directory
- Features: Professor contact info, university staff finder

2.9 PEER CONNECT (PeerConnect.tsx) - Route: /peer-connect [PROTECTED]
- Social networking for students
- Features: Study group finder, cross-department connections
- Component: Peer connection network

2.10 FEEDBACK (Feedback.tsx) - Route: /feedback [PROTECTED]
- User feedback collection system
- Security: Only users can submit feedback on themselves

2.11 LAYOUT COMPONENT (Layout.tsx)
- Wrapper for all protected routes
- Includes: Sidebar navigation, user menu, theme controls
- Sidebar items: Dashboard, AI Study Helper, Peer Connect, AI Chat, Map, 
              Announcements, Faculty, Feedback
- Features: Mobile responsive menu toggle, logout, account deletion

================================================================================
3. TECHNICAL STACK
================================================================================

3.1 FRONTEND
- Framework: React 19 (with React DOM 19)
- Language: TypeScript 5.8.2
- Styling: 
  * Tailwind CSS 4.1.14 (@tailwindcss/vite)
  * Custom CSS (index.css)
  * Utilities: clsx, tailwind-merge for className management
- Build Tool: Vite 6.2.3
- Build Plugin: @vitejs/plugin-react 5.0.4

3.2 ANIMATION & UI
- Framer Motion: 12.38.0 (legacy)
- Motion: 12.23.24 (new Framer Motion)
- Icons: Lucide React 0.546.0
- Used for: Smooth transitions, motion components, AnimatePresence

3.3 ROUTING
- React Router: 7.15.0
- Protected routes via ProtectedRoute component
- Routes configured in App.tsx

3.4 BACKEND
- Runtime: Node.js with Express 4.21.2
- Server File: server.ts (runs via tsx during dev, esbuild for production)
- Port: 3000 (0.0.0.0)
- Development: npm run dev (tsx server.ts)
- Production: npm run build && npm start

3.5 DATABASE
- Primary: MongoDB (via Mongoose 9.6.2)
- Fallback: In-memory store when DB unavailable (demo mode)
- Connection: connectDB() in src/lib/mongodb.ts
- Model: User schema (User.ts)

3.6 AUTHENTICATION & SECURITY
- JWT: jsonwebtoken 9.0.3
- Password Hashing: bcryptjs 3.0.3
- Token Secret: JWT_SECRET from .env or fallback 'nexus-quantum-secret-key-2024'
- Expiry: 7 days per token

3.7 AI INTEGRATION
- Provider: Google Gemini API
- Package: @google/genai 1.29.0
- Model: gemini-3-flash-preview
- API Key: Environment variable GEMINI_API_KEY
- Helper: src/lib/gemini.ts with generateGeminiContent() function

3.8 ENVIRONMENT & BUILD
- Environment: dotenv 17.4.2 for .env file management
- Build Tool: esbuild 0.25.0 (for server bundling)
- Compiler: tsx 4.21.0 (TypeScript runtime)

3.9 CONFIGURATION FILES
- tsconfig.json: ES2022 target, React JSX, path aliases (@/*)
- vite.config.ts: Tailwind, React plugins, HMR configuration
- package.json: Scripts for dev, build, start, clean, lint

================================================================================
4. PROJECT STRUCTURE
================================================================================

ROOT DIRECTORY:
├── index.html              - HTML entry point
├── package.json            - Dependencies and scripts
├── tsconfig.json           - TypeScript configuration
├── vite.config.ts          - Vite build configuration
├── server.ts               - Express server with auth routes
├── README.md               - Project documentation
├── security_spec.md        - Security specifications
├── metadata.json           - Project metadata
├── PROJECT_SUMMARY.txt     - This file

SRC/ DIRECTORY:
├── main.tsx                - React app entry point
├── App.tsx                 - Main app component with routing
├── index.css               - Global styles
│
├── components/
│   └── Layout.tsx          - Sidebar, navigation, wrapper component
│
├── context/
│   └── AuthContext.tsx     - Global authentication context
│                            (user state, login, signup, logout, deleteAccount)
│
├── lib/
│   ├── mongodb.ts          - MongoDB connection handler
│   └── gemini.ts           - Google Gemini AI integration
│
├── models/
│   └── User.ts             - Mongoose User schema
│
└── pages/
    ├── Landing.tsx         - Public landing page
    ├── Login.tsx           - Login form
    ├── Signup.tsx          - Signup form
    ├── Dashboard.tsx       - Main dashboard [PROTECTED]
    ├── AIChat.tsx          - AI chat interface [PROTECTED]
    ├── StudyHelper.tsx     - Study helper page [PROTECTED]
    ├── CampusMap.tsx       - Campus map page [PROTECTED]
    ├── Announcements.tsx   - Announcements feed [PROTECTED]
    ├── Faculty.tsx         - Faculty directory [PROTECTED]
    ├── PeerConnect.tsx     - Peer connection network [PROTECTED]
    └── Feedback.tsx        - Feedback form [PROTECTED]

================================================================================
5. API ENDPOINTS
================================================================================

5.1 HEALTH CHECK
- GET /api/health
- Response: { status, university, dbConnected, mode, uriPresent }
- Purpose: Check DB connection and system status

5.2 AUTHENTICATION
- POST /api/auth/signup
  Body: { name, email, password, major, role }
  Response: { user, token }
  Error: 400 if user exists, 500 on server error

- POST /api/auth/login
  Body: { email, password }
  Response: { user, token }
  Error: 401 for invalid credentials, 500 on server error

5.3 USER DATA
- User password is NEVER sent to frontend (deleted from response)
- User sessions stored in localStorage with keys:
  * nexus_token: JWT token
  * nexus_user: { uid, email }
  * nexus_profile: Full user profile object

================================================================================
6. SECURITY & AUTHENTICATION FLOW
================================================================================

6.1 USER AUTHENTICATION
1. User signs up with name, email, password, major, role
2. Password hashed with bcryptjs (10 salt rounds)
3. User created in MongoDB or memory store (if DB down)
4. JWT token generated (expires in 7 days)
5. Token + user data sent to frontend

6.2 LOGIN FLOW
1. User submits email + password
2. Email lookup in DB or memory
3. Password compared with bcrypt.compare()
4. JWT token generated on success
5. Token stored in localStorage

6.3 PROTECTED ROUTES
- All routes except /, /login, /signup require authentication
- ProtectedRoute component in App.tsx checks user state
- Redirects to /login if no user
- Routes wrapped in <ProtectedRoute> tags

6.4 SECURITY SPECS
- Users can only access/update their own data
- Students cannot change their role to admin
- Only admins can create announcements
- Feedback only for own user
- Timestamps server-generated

================================================================================
7. DATABASE MODEL
================================================================================

USER SCHEMA (MongoDB/Mongoose):
{
  _id: ObjectId (auto-generated),
  name: String (required),
  email: String (required, unique),
  password: String (required, hashed),
  major: String (default: 'Undecided'),
  role: String (default: 'Student'),
  year: String (default: '1st'),
  interests: [String],
  bio: String (optional),
  createdAt: Date (default: Date.now)
}

Roles: 'student', 'faculty', 'admin'

================================================================================
8. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
================================================================================

REQUIRED:
- MONGODB_URI: MongoDB connection string (optional, fallback to memory)
- GEMINI_API_KEY: Google Gemini API key (required for AI chat)
- JWT_SECRET: Secret key for JWT signing (fallback provided)
- NODE_ENV: 'development' or 'production'
- DISABLE_HMR: Set to 'true' to disable Vite HMR (for AI Studio)

================================================================================
9. BUILD & DEPLOYMENT
================================================================================

DEVELOPMENT:
- npm install: Install dependencies
- npm run dev: Start dev server (tsx server.ts)
- Server runs on http://localhost:3000
- Vite handles React HMR

PRODUCTION:
- npm run build: Build React (Vite) + Bundle server (esbuild)
  * Outputs: dist/ with React bundle + server.cjs
- npm start: Run production server (node dist/server.cjs)
- Vite serves static files from dist/

CLEANING:
- npm run clean: Remove dist/ directory
- npm run lint: Run TypeScript type check (tsc --noEmit)

================================================================================
10. KEY DEPENDENCIES & VERSIONS
================================================================================

CORE FRAMEWORKS:
- react@19.0.1
- react-dom@19.0.1
- react-router-dom@7.15.0
- express@4.21.2
- mongoose@9.6.2

STYLING & UI:
- tailwindcss@4.1.14
- @tailwindcss/vite@4.1.14
- framer-motion@12.38.0
- motion@12.23.24
- lucide-react@0.546.0
- clsx@2.1.1
- tailwind-merge@3.6.0

AUTHENTICATION:
- jsonwebtoken@9.0.3
- bcryptjs@3.0.3

AI:
- @google/genai@1.29.0

BUILD & DEVELOPMENT:
- vite@6.2.3
- @vitejs/plugin-react@5.0.4
- typescript@5.8.2
- tsx@4.21.0
- esbuild@0.25.0
- dotenv@17.4.2

================================================================================
11. DESIGN & STYLING
================================================================================

COLOR PALETTE:
- Quantum Cyber Cyan (Primary): #00f2ff
- Electric Purple (Accent): #8a2be2
- Deep Obsidian Background: Dark theme
- Text colors: white, white/60 (secondary), white/10 (tertiary)

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Outfit: Bold futuristic headings
- Inter: Clean UI text
- JetBrains Mono: Technical/data display

VISUAL EFFECTS:
- Glassmorphism: Semi-transparent glass effect
- Neon Glow: Primary color glow-text class
- Radial Gradients: Background styling
- Smooth Transitions: Via Motion/Framer Motion
- Cyan/Purple Neon: Cyberpunk aesthetic

CSS CLASSES:
- glass: Glassmorphism effect
- glow-text: Neon cyan glow on text
- primary, accent: Color variables
- text-primary, text-accent: Themed text colors

================================================================================
12. KEY FUNCTIONS & UTILITIES
================================================================================

AUTHENTICATION (AuthContext.tsx):
- login(email, password): Authenticate user
- signup(userData): Create new user account
- logout(): Clear session
- deleteAccount(): Permanently delete user
- resetDatabase(): Clear all data (demo)

DATABASE (mongodb.ts):
- connectDB(): Initialize MongoDB connection with fallback

AI INTEGRATION (gemini.ts):
- getGeminiAI(): Singleton instance of GoogleGenAI
- generateGeminiContent(prompt, history, context): Call Gemini API
- SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION: Global AI system prompt

COMPONENTS (Layout.tsx):
- Sidebar with navigation links
- Mobile-responsive menu
- User profile dropdown
- Logout functionality
- Account deletion

================================================================================
13. IMPORTANT NOTES
================================================================================

1. FALLBACK MODE: If MongoDB is unavailable, the system uses in-memory storage
   for demo purposes. Production requires valid MONGODB_URI.

2. AI API DEPENDENCY: AI Chat feature requires valid GEMINI_API_KEY. Without it,
   generateGeminiContent() will throw an error.

3. JWT EXPIRY: Tokens expire after 7 days. Users need to re-login.

4. PROTECTED ROUTES: All routes except /, /login, /signup redirect unauthenticated
   users to /login.

5. ROLE-BASED ACCESS: Currently authenticated, but role enforcement is defined
   in security_spec.md (admin-only features not fully enforced on frontend).

6. DEVELOPMENT MODE: Vite HMR is enabled by default (can be disabled with DISABLE_HMR).

7. BUILD ARTIFACTS: Production build creates dist/ with:
   - React bundle (index.html + assets)
   - Server executable (server.cjs)

8. ERROR HANDLING: Auth errors (invalid credentials, user exists) return JSON errors.
   AI errors are displayed to user in chat interface.

================================================================================
14. QUICK REFERENCE COMMANDS
================================================================================

npm run dev                 → Start development server
npm run build              → Build for production
npm run start              → Start production server
npm run clean              → Clean build artifacts
npm run lint               → TypeScript type checking

API CALLS (in code):
POST /api/auth/signup      → Create account
POST /api/auth/login       → Login user
GET /api/health            → Check system status

KEY FILES TO REMEMBER:
- src/App.tsx              → Main routing setup
- src/context/AuthContext  → Global auth state
- server.ts                → Backend server + API routes
- src/lib/gemini.ts        → AI integration
- src/models/User.ts       → Database schema

================================================================================
END OF PROJECT SUMMARY
================================================================================
